verifier.py checks for exactly 3 clusters (c2 += 20 if n_clusters == 3)
verifier.py uses metadata.get('min_price_ratio', 1.5) and checks if max/min ratio >= 1.5

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real San Francisco residential building data from UDST/sanfran_urbansim. Analysis of the downloaded HDF5 dataset shows 152,605 total buildings, with exactly 138,407 eligible for clustering (having residential_units > 0, building_sqft > 0, and residential_sales_price > 0). The median calculated price_per_sqft across eligible buildings is ~0.30 (prices are likely represented in thousands). The analysis strictly requires creating exactly 3 clusters with a minimum price ratio (max to min mean price) of at least 1.5. Output validation requires the number of rows in the cluster CSV to be within 50% to 150% of the true eligible count (138,407), and expects three specific files: a building cluster CSV, a profile CSV, and a scatterplot PNG.
